items.title: ACT minister reassures NSW patients after one claims care was denied

children.content: ACT Health Minister Rachel Stephen-Smith says a misunderstanding led a NSW patient to believe they had been denied healthcare in Canberra because they lived over the border.

image.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

items.title: Driver charged over fiery multi-truck crash near Goulburn

children.content: A 28-year-old semi-trailer driver is charged with negligent driving over an explosive collision that saw six trucks destroyed in a fire at a rest stop near Goulburn.

image.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

items.title: ANU head apologises for failing to protect Jewish staff, students

children.content: The interim vice-chancellor of the Australian National University has apologised for the institution's failure to protect the psychological safety of Jewish students and staff during pro-Palestinian encampments in 2024.

gallery.altText: Tents set up with buildings in the background on the ANU campus.

thumbnail.altText: Tents set up with buildings in the background on the ANU campus.
A woman with long blonde hair wearing a black blazer stands in a conference room looking serious.

items.title: Man denied assisted dying wins partial victory in early test of ACT law

children.content: A tribunal has ruled on a man's bid to overturn his rejection from the ACT's assisted dying scheme in one of the first tests of how people can challenge such decisions.

image.altText: A building sign that reads 'ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal'.

items.title: Former ACT politician to stand trial on grooming charges

children.content: Former ACT Attorney-General Gordon Ramsay is committed to stand trial for allegedly grooming a teenage boy between 2022 and 2025.

image.altText: Gordon Ramsay walks outside the court in a suit and tie.

items.title: 'Very special' oval that hosts footy, weddings set for First Nations control

children.content: The transition of ownership is part of a government agreement with the ACT Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elected Body.

items.title: NSW scrapped this 'frustrating' road rule. Should the ACT follow?

children.content: The NRMA is encouraging all states and territories to follow the lead of NSW in removing reduced speed limits for roadworks outside construction hours.

image.altText: A temporary sign saying Roadwork Ahead and a 40 kph speed limit.

items.title: As the mercury drops in Canberra, cyclists are still braving the chill

children.content: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

image.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

items.title: Starmer says political journey 'over' as he faces last question session

children.content: Outgoing UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation last month, has taken part in his last weekly grilling from MPs in parliament.

image.altText: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer stands at a microphone to speak inside the historic House of Commons.

items.title: BHP workers to strike as mining giant hits record production

children.content: As hundreds of BHP workers prepare to down tools in a landmark strike, the miner has revealed its highest-ever levels of iron ore production.

image.altText: An image of iron ore operations including loaders and conveyer belts

items.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about fuel prices as war in Iran escalates again

children.content: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

articles.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iranian targets 'threatening' Strait shipping

children.content: US Central Command says it launched a 90-minute attack targeting Iran's strategic Greater Tunb Island.

image.altText: Black and white aerial view of explosion.

articles.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

articles.title: Iran frees US citizen after almost two years of imprisonment

children.content: Iran has freed a US-Iranian citizen detained since December, 2024 in what Trump says was a 'gesture of goodwill'.

image.altText: People walk near an anti-U.S. mural in Tehran, Iran, July 15, 2026.

gallery.altText: Smoke rises in Chabahar, Iran, in this screengrab taken from a social media video, released July 15, 2026.

items.title: Hegseth unveils plan to screen troops for low testosterone

children.content: Under the policy US troops will be offered the chance to undergo testosterone replacement therapy.

image.altText: A close-up of Pete Hegseth in a blue suit.

items.title: Tamworth music festival manager quits after sexual touching admission

children.content: Joel Ulbricht admitted he had sexually touched someone without consent in January this year.

image.altText: A middle-aged man looks into camera with a neutral expression and hands in pockets.

items.title: You'll now be able to spot an AI-generated song on Spotify

children.content: Streaming giant Spotify has stopped short of expunging artificially generated music from its platform, but it is making it easier for listeners to work out that they are listening to tracks produced using AI.

articles.title: Community meets over plans for large 'AI factory' at River Murray town

children.content: An AI company says its proposed projects have the potential to create hundreds of jobs in South Australia, but residents of one River Murray town say they want to know more about the potential impacts.

image.altText: Tailem Bend locals at a public forum.

articles.title: Albanese rolls out rule book and red carpet for AI companies

children.content: If Australia is truly going to coax major AI companies to choose us rather than the other suitor nations with similar ambitions, some tough decisions need to be made soon.

image.altText: Two men and a woman in suits stand at a podium speaking to journalists with the Australian flag in the background.

articles.title: Artists seek ‘seat at table’ in AI office

children.content: The creative sector hopes there is a space to contribute to the Office of AI, given its content powers a lot of these tools.

image.altText: A woman in black sits in front of an ARIA sign.

gallery.altText: Sam Duboff wearing a green shirt standing in front of a Spotify sign.

thumbnail.altText: Sam Duboff wearing a green shirt standing in front of a Spotify sign.

items.title: Australian brand has a 'Trump account' after six-figure tariff refund

children.content: Businesses globally are clawing back tens of billions of dollars paid to United States customs after Donald Trump's so-called liberation day tariffs were deemed unlawful.

image.altText: a man in a warehouse smiling

items.title: Q-Day is coming and it might break the entire internet

children.content: The countdown is on to Q-Day, when an algorithm written more than 30 years ago could run on a quantum computer and compromise the security of data on the internet. How are we preparing?

image.altText: Woman with laptop holding her bank card while typing. Close up of computer and hands only.

items.title: Too many cooks spoil The Odyssey's bubbling epic

children.content: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

gallery.altText: A man dressed as a solider stands in front of a crowd and ships.
Robert Pattinson is one of the side characters offered something meatier as murderous suitor Antinous.

thumbnail.altText: A man dressed as a solider stands in front of a crowd and ships.
Anne Hathaway dressed as a Greek princess on a film set

items.title: I thought I could use voodoo to win a TV competition, instead I won back my joy

children.content: Halfway through the Race Around The World competition, I'm second last on the leaderboard. So, I decide to cheat.

image.altText: A voodoo doll of Kate.

items.title: From Mozart to the Beatles, prove you're a music genius with this quiz

children.content: From famous icons and musical terms to unusual instruments and classical influences in popular music, test your knowledge with this quiz.

image.altText: Celine Dion in a black leather jacket sings into a handheld microphone under warm yellow stage lights during a concert.

items.title: Cyclist bitten by brown snake caught in her bike chain

children.content: The woman, in her 60s, was riding on a northern NSW rail trail when she ran over a two-metre snake that became trapped then bit her.

items.title: ASX rises as SpaceX shares see $1 trillion wiped

children.content: The Aussie share market opens up as SpaceX shares see more than $1 trillion wiped. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Country footballer handed life ban after opponents' complaint

children.content: A player and assistant coach for a southern NSW AFL team has been handed a "life ban" following a tribunal hearing into eight offences.

image.altText: A player in an AFL guernsey doing a ridgy didge hand signal

items.title: Images of clothes released to identify alleged childcare abuse victims

children.content: Images of distinctive clothing have been released as authorities attempt to identify victims of a Sydney childcare worker who allegedly abused more than 130 children.

items.title: Telstra and telco regulator prepare for grilling over widespread outage

children.content: Telstra prepares for a grilling in parliament about the recent network outage that disrupted Australia, with the communications regulator also set to face tough questions.

image.altText: A woman in a blazer holds a document and looks serious.

items.title: Accused gangster to call on AFP in controversial deportation case

children.content: Lawyers for accused underworld figure Masood Zakaria want to call AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett to give evidence as he seeks to have his murder charges thrown out of court.

image.altText: A blonde woman in a navy police officer uniform.

children.content: Train operators in rural Canada were trapped by raging wildfires, as nearby communities were evacuated.

items.title: Gold Coast destroys 30,000 fire ant nests but confidence remains 'pretty low'

children.content: Acting Mayor Mark Hammel says he is "alarmed and pleased" by the number of nests destroyed.

image.altText: mound of dirt with ants on it

items.title: Ex-MP says misconduct charge ruined his career and his children's lives

children.content: Former NSW Liberal government minister John Sidoti says the impacts of allegations of misconduct while in public office have been "disgraceful" and "horrible".

image.altText: John Sidoti arrives at court wearing a suit and tie

items.title: Ancestral remains begin long journey home after 143 years at museum

children.content: The remains of members of the Rapa Nui people from Easter Island are "finally going home" after sitting in the Australian Museum's collection for over a century.

gallery.altText: Repatriation of Rapa Nui ancestral remains from Australian Museum

thumbnail.altText: Repatriation of Rapa Nui ancestral remains from Australian Museum

items.title: Scars of alleged Invasion Day terror attack run deep six months on

children.content: A day of evidence reflecting on racism, hate and violence directed towards Aboriginal people highlights the ongoing impact of what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" at a Perth rally.

image.altText: About a dozen police vehicles parked across a road to close it off.

items.title: 'I want the money that is owed to me': Charity fails to pay $150k in super

children.content: Former staff of charity PAWS Darwin are speaking out about being short-changed on their superannuation to the tune of $150,000. Under-18 staff also say the super accounts PAWS promised to create for them never existed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a white woman, with blonde hair tied back, black singlet top, sitting among greenery.

items.title: What it takes to care for the world’s fastest bird, including saying sorry to their food

children.content: Peregrine falcons and wedge-tailed eagles could be among the first native species to contract a strain of bird flu that has recently arrived in Australia.
